heading: Congressional declaration of policy and purpose
label: 1331 Congressional declaration of policy and purpose
notes: Amendments1984—Par. (1). substituted “about any adverse health effects of cigarette smoking by inclusion of warning notices on each package of cigarettes and in each advertisement;” for “that cigarette smoking may be hazardous to health by inclusion of a warning to that effect on each package of cigarettes;”.Pub. L. 98–4741970— reenacted section without change.Pub. L. 91–222

Effective Date of 1970 Amendment, , , provided in part that:Pub. L. 91–222, § 384 Stat. 90Apr. 1, 1970“All other provisions of the amendment made by this Act [enacting , amending this section and sections 1332 and 1335 to 1339 of this title, and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section] except where otherwise specified shall take effect on .”section 1340 of this titleJanuary 1, 1970

Effective Date, formerly § 11, , , as renumbered by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 89–92, § 1279 Stat. 284Pub. L. 98–474, § 5(a)98 Stat. 2203July 27, 1965Oct. 12, 1984“This Act [this chapter] shall take effect on .”January 1, 1966

Short Title of 1984 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–474, § 198 Stat. 2200Oct. 12, 1984“This Act [enacting sections 1335a and 1341 of this title, amending this section and sections 1332, 1333, 1336, and 1337 of this title, and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section and sections 1333 and 1335a of this title] may be cited as the ‘Comprehensive Smoking Education Act’.”

Short Title of 1973 Amendment, , , provided:Pub. L. 93–109, § 187 Stat. 352Sept. 21, 1973“That this Act [amending sections 1332 and 1335 of this title] may be cited as the ‘Little Cigar Act of 1973’.”

Short Title of 1970 Amendment, , , provided:Pub. L. 91–222, § 184 Stat. 87Apr. 1, 1970“That this Act [enacting , amending this section and sections 1332 to 1339 of this title, and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section and sections 1333 and 1334 of this title] may be cited as the ‘Public Health Cigarette Smoking Act of 1969’.”section 1340 of this title
Short Title, , , provided:Pub. L. 89–92, § 179 Stat. 282July 27, 1965“This Act [enacting this chapter] may be cited as the ‘Federal Cigarette Labeling and Advertising Act’.”

Separability, formerly § 12, as added by , , , and renumbered , , , provided that:Pub. L. 89–92, § 13Pub. L. 91–222, § 284 Stat. 90Pub. L. 98–474, § 5(a)98 Stat. 2203Apr. 1, 1970Oct. 12, 1984“If any provision of this Act [this chapter] or the application thereof to any person or circumstances is held invalid, the other provisions of this Act [this chapter] and the application of such provisions to other persons or circumstances shall not be affected thereby.”

Congressional Statement of Purpose, , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–474, § 298 Stat. 2200Oct. 12, 1984“It is the purpose of this Act [see Short Title of 1984 Amendment note above] to provide a new strategy for making Americans more aware of any adverse health effects of smoking, to assure the timely and widespread dissemination of research findings and to enable individuals to make informed decisions about smoking.”

children.text: the public may be adequately informed about any adverse health effects of cigarette smoking by inclusion of warning notices on each package of cigarettes and in each advertisement of cigarettes; and

commerce and the national economy may be (A) protected to the maximum extent consistent with this declared policy and (B) not impeded by diverse, nonuniform, and confusing cigarette labeling and advertising regulations with respect to any relationship between smoking and health.
